_comment.1: Open-loop plant Bode of the x/tilt axis: command -> FSM -> camera -> CoM.
_comment.2: SOFTWARE injection: bode_plot replaces the x element (1; CoM is [y, x])
_comment.3: with a sine each iteration, and piplate_bridge writes offset + sine*scale
_comment.4: to DAC ch1 every cycle. Camera/CoM settings match the current steering
_comment.5: geometry (56x32, exp 50, gain 100). Whole-frame CoM, no
_comment.6: tracking/acquisition: bode_plot injects from the first frame, so there
_comment.7: must be no acquisition phase contaminating the first sweep points.
_comment.8: No PID is in the loop, so the command IS the sine = open loop. Phase is
_comment.9: measured from wall-clock time and includes the full transport delay
_comment.10: (measured separately: ~1.7 ms at this frame rate).
_comment.11: baud 921600: the port is USB CDC so the wire ignores baud, but the
_comment.12: bridge paces itself by it -- at 115200 it would cap at ~440 writes/s
_comment.13: and mangle the injected sine at a 2310 Hz loop rate.
_comment.14: y/tip (ch0) and the coarse channels are parked at their bias voltages.

params._note: tracked 15x15 window, threshold 30 -- matches steering_tuned.json exactly so the sweep measures the same sensor the loop sees. Initialized on the beam (frame centre) with no acquisition phase: bode_plot injects from the first frame, and the stray reflection at ROI (10,28) must stay out of the sum. Output is image-normalized.

pipeline._comment: Replace the x/tilt element (1) with the swept sine; read CoM x as response

params._tuning: n_freqs 100 / n_settle 400 / n_cycles 40 (2026-07-14): the 240-pt/12-cycle sweep was jagged -- 12 cycles is only ~0.12 s of correlation at 100 Hz, so ambient lines and CoM drift don't average out (estimator error falls as 1/n_cycles for line/DC leakage). 40 cycles also makes the 5 Hz window 8 s long, resolving it from the 5.64 Hz line. Total ~3.5 min.

pipeline._note: same mapping as steering_tuned.json: ch0=y(idx0, parked), ch1=x(idx1, driven), ch2/ch3 coarse parked. skip_unchanged: only the driven channel costs wire time. Coarse offsets 1.95/2.02 = current operating point (2026-07-16 evening, 56x32 setup: beam parks at the default-centred ROI, no start_x/start_y needed) so the sweep measures the plant the tuned loop actually sees; earlier operating points: 2.52/1.62 (07-14), 2.7/1.65, 2.6/1.4 (07-13), 1.05/2.35 (attenuation runs), 0.8/2.394 (07-10, |P|x=0.43).
